steps.title: Identify which metrics to compare
steps.content: The statement asks us to evaluate data on two separate metrics from the table: total cars produced and the number of companies producing cars. We need to track whether each one increased, decreased, or remained constant from 1910 to 1925.

steps.title: Read the data and evaluate the choices
steps.content:
Reading the table: 1910 had 123,990 cars from 320 companies; 1925 had 3,185,881 cars from 80 companies. Choice A correctly captures this pattern: cars produced increased dramatically while the number of companies decreased sharply.

The other three fail because they either claim both stayed constant, reverse the production trend, or incorrectly claim companies grew.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
